description: Empidonax flycatcher with clear lemon-yellow throat and upper chest fading into pale ochre-yellow belly and vent with some olive wash at sides of lower chest. Overall structure rather reminiscent of Least Flycatcher, with largish flattish head, shortish tail, moderate primary extension. The bill was perhaps a bit larger than that of a Least and entirely orangish beneath. Base color of wings blackish with contrasting white tertial and secondary edges and white wingbars. Back was uniformly deep emerald green through crown and into auriculars, including nape, giving almost a helmeted appearance different from other Empids, which usually have some gray wash on nape, head or auriculars. Green auriculars thus sharply cut off from lemon yellow throat. Eyering round and washed yellow. Viewed at close range for about 45 seconds by me, longer by DaveBehaviors: The bird had the habit of repeatedly flicking its wings and tail.Call: not heardPlumage: fall adult
